compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can rely on a scope-drift reviewer agent to classify fix drift as no-drift, minor-drift, or major-drift
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Create the dso:scope-drift-reviewer agent definition — a sonnet-level agent that receives a bug ticket, investigation report, and git diff, then classifies whether the fix stays within the ticket's behavioral scope.
**Why**: Bug fixes currently pass through code review without any check for whether they exceed the ticket's stated scope. This agent is the core new capability for detecting behavioral drift.
**Scope**:
- IN: Agent definition file (plugins/dso/agents/scope-drift-reviewer.md), input contract (ticket text, root_cause_report, git diff, investigation files), PARSED_SCOPE checkpoint with strict read order, drift classification logic (no-drift/minor-drift/major-drift), enumerated heuristic table for behavioral vs non-behavioral boundary, output signal schema conforming to gate-signal-schema.md
- OUT: Integration into fix-bug SKILL.md (Story 2), config keys, escalation router changes

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the scope-drift-reviewer agent definition exists at plugins/dso/agents/scope-drift-reviewer.md with model: sonnet, and includes the PARSED_SCOPE checkpoint that commits ticket scope extraction before processing the root_cause_report
  ← Satisfies: "When a fix introduces behavioral changes not described in the ticket, the scope-drift reviewer classifies them as major drift"
- When this story is complete, the agent emits a gate signal with gate_id: scope_drift classifying drift as no-drift, minor-drift, or major-drift, following the enumerated heuristic table that distinguishes behavioral changes (executed code path changes) from non-behavioral changes (renames, comments, dead-code removal)
  ← Satisfies: "When a fix includes non-behavioral changes adjacent to the fix, the scope-drift reviewer classifies as minor drift, emits a warning"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Depends on ticket text quality — agent must emit scope_insufficient and halt when ticket contains fewer than 2 extractable behavioral claims, rather than proceeding with inferred scope
- [Testing] Agent behavioral tests needed — verify classification accuracy with synthetic ticket/diff pairs covering edge cases (renames, type annotation changes, constant value changes)
- [Maintainability] Gate signal must conform to gate-signal-schema.md — use gate_id: scope_drift, signal_type: primary

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): gate-signal-schema.md consumers table must be updated to register scope_drift gate_id. The contract requires atomic updates when new gates are added.
comments.author: Test
